triangulation.title: SAR Scanner - Device Triangulation
triangulation.macPlaceholder: Enter a valid MAC address (e.g., AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF)
triangulation.noSightings: No sightings found for this device

tooltips.toggleTooltips: Toggle help tooltips on buttons and controls. Turn on to see descriptions of what each button does when you hover over it.
tooltips.darkModeToggle: Switch between light and dark color themes. Dark mode is easier on the eyes in low-light conditions and reduces screen glare.
tooltips.pauseResumeBtn: Pause or resume live data updates. When paused, the display freezes so you can analyze the current data without it changing. Click again to resume real-time updates.
tooltips.analyzeConfidenceBtn: Run the confidence analysis algorithm on all detected devices. This calculates how likely each device belongs to a real person vs. being random/spurious based on signal patterns, timing, and behavior.
tooltips.updateOuiBtn: Download the latest OUI (Organizationally Unique Identifier) vendor database. This helps identify device manufacturers from MAC addresses for better device classification.
tooltips.downloadDbBtn: Download the entire scanner database as a file. Use this to backup your data, transfer it to another system, or analyze it offline with external tools.
tooltips.purgeDbBtn: Delete ALL data from the database. A backup copy will be created first. Use this to start fresh when beginning a new search operation. WARNING: This action cannot be undone!
tooltips.clearUsbBtn: Delete ALL files from the USB storage device. Use this to clear exported data and free up space on the USB drive. WARNING: This action cannot be undone!
tooltips.updateBtn: Update the SAR Scanner software from the Git repository. This downloads and installs the latest version with bug fixes and new features. The system may restart after update.
tooltips.rebootBtn: Restart the scanner system immediately. All active connections will be disconnected. Use this if the system becomes unresponsive or after configuration changes.
tooltips.shutdownBtn: Safely shut down the scanner system. You will need to physically power it back on. Use this before disconnecting power to prevent data corruption.
tooltips.tabBtDevices: View the list of detected Bluetooth devices. Shows MAC address, device name, manufacturer, confidence score, and when each device was last seen.
tooltips.tabBtSightings: View individual Bluetooth signal detections (sightings). Shows each time a device was detected with its signal strength and timestamp. Useful for tracking device movement.
tooltips.tabWifiDevices: View the list of detected WiFi devices (stations). Shows MAC address, vendor, device type, confidence score, and last seen time.
tooltips.tabWifiAssoc: View WiFi association events. Shows which networks (SSIDs) each device has tried to connect to, packet types, signal strength, and timestamps.
tooltips.mapBtBtn: Show only Bluetooth device locations on the heatmap. Filters the map to display where BT devices were detected.
tooltips.mapWifiBtn: Show only WiFi device locations on the heatmap. Filters the map to display where WiFi devices were detected.
tooltips.mapAllBtn: Show both Bluetooth and WiFi device locations on the heatmap. Combines all detection data for a complete view of device activity.
tooltips.timePreset1h: Filter data to show only the last 1 hour. Useful for focusing on recently detected devices and current activity.
tooltips.timePreset6h: Filter data to show only the last 6 hours. Good balance between recent activity and historical context.
tooltips.timePreset24h: Filter data to show only the last 24 hours. Shows a full day of scanning activity.
tooltips.timePresetAll: Show all data without time filtering. Displays every detection since the database was created or last purged.
tooltips.timeRangeApply: Apply the custom date/time range to filter data. Set the start and end times above, then click to filter.
tooltips.aboutBtn: Open the SAR Scanner project page on GitHub. View documentation, report issues, or contribute to the project.
tooltips.rssiMinSlider: Set minimum RSSI (signal strength) filter. Lower values include weaker signals from distant devices.
tooltips.rssiMaxSlider: Set maximum RSSI (signal strength) filter. Higher values include only stronger signals from nearby devices.
tooltips.confidenceMinSlider: Set minimum confidence score filter. Higher values show only devices more likely to be real persons.
tooltips.confidenceMaxSlider: Set maximum confidence score filter. Lower values exclude high-confidence devices.
tooltips.macFilter: Filter devices by MAC address. Type part of a MAC address to find specific devices. Supports partial matching.
tooltips.ssidFilter: Filter WiFi associations by network name (SSID). Type part of a network name to find devices with matching connections.
